channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Marina Ussía se une a AIG como directora de Operaciones para EMEA
item.description: Desde la pasada semana, como ella misma anuncia en su perfil en Linkedin, Marina Ussía ha comenzado un nuevo puesto como directora de Operaciones – Prestación de Servicios de AIG para EMEA. Llega procedente de Everest, donde ha trabajado los últimos dos años y medio en funciones también de gestión de operaciones. Previamente, se desempeñó durante casi 10 años en distintos cargos de responsabilidad en Chubb.

item.title: Las aseguradoras refuerzan la gestión de riesgos epidémicos tras las lecciones de la Covid-19
item.description: AM Best ha publicado un informe en el que concluye que las aseguradoras están en condiciones de gestionar el impacto directo de ambos eventos, en parte por los ajustes realizados tras la pandemia de Covid-19. La agencia recomienda a las aseguradoras integrar los brotes de enfermedades en la gestión de riesgos corporativos y realizar pruebas de estrés periódicas sobre riesgos no modelizados. Estas medidas, según la agencia de calificación, deben contribuir a limitar las pérdidas directas y a reforzar la capacidad de respuesta ante riesgos epidémicos o pandémicos más sistémicos.

item.title: La Salud como palanca transformadora: «No hay que separar a las personas de la estrategia»
item.description: Con esta mirada transversal sobre la necesidad de medir la salud de una compañía, afirma que “no se debe separar a las personas de la estrategia”. Durante años la salud y los beneficios se han visto como un coste; hoy se entienden cada vez más como una palanca real del negocio: impactan en la productividad, en el compromiso y en la sostenibilidad del talento. Y lo más importante: no separar a las personas de la estrategia.

item.title: ‘Decisive Sign of a Softened Market’: Premiums Decrease Across All Accounts
item.description: During the first three months of 2026, The Council of Insurance Agents & Brokers (CIAB)’s market survey found premiums decreased an average of 1.2% across all accounts. For the first time since the third quarter 2017, respondents to a recent quarterly survey said average premiums decreased across all account sizes. Small account premiums increased 1.1%, a 60% decrease from Q4 2025’s rise of 2.8%.

item.title: AI You Can Defend: What High-Stakes Claims Demand from AI
item.description: High-stakes claims rarely follow a tidy narrative. AI medical record review tools designed for high-stakes environments need to deliver structured, source-cited outputs that hold up under challenge from the earliest stages of review. View Series AI is now woven into everyday claims workflows, yet scrutiny around its use is rising just as quickly.

item.title: PLRB and Spencer Educational Foundation Announce 2026 PLRB Scholarship Recipients
item.description: (Spencer) is pleased to announce that Karla Iturrubiates and Lauren Ribbe have been named recipients of the 2026 PLRB Scholarship. The Property & Liability Resource Bureau (PLRB), in partnership with the Spencer Educational Foundation, Inc. Those interested in supporting future scholarship growth can learn more at: Spencer Educational Foundation .

item.title: Zurich’s US$11bn Beazley deal draws Australian antitrust scrutiny over ‘competitive overlap’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Levies on general insurers risk exacerbating insurance affordability pressures: ICA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Asia emerges as cyber growth frontier amid rising ransomware threats: CyberCube-UIB report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA to hold Annual Public Meeting in Edinburgh
item.description: We’re pleased to announce that our Annual Public Meeting (APM) will be held in Edinburgh for the first time on 6 October 2026, marking an important milestone for us a UK-wide regulator. Ashley Alder, chair of the FCA, said: 'Bringing our Annual Public Meeting to Edinburgh for the first time is a deliberate step to strengthen our visibility and accountability right across the UK. Holding the APM in Edinburgh, reflects our focus on being more outward-facing, more engaged, and better connected to the markets we serve.' Chris Cummings, chief executive of the Investment Association said: 'Edinburgh has a long heritage as a leading centre for financial services, including investment management, and it’s great to see that recognised as the city hosts the FCA’s Annual Public Meeting.

item.title: LGT reinsurer Lumen Re maintains its lower loss ratio, premiums written increasing
item.description: Lumen Re, the main Bermuda-domiciled rated reinsurance underwriting vehicle of specialist insurance-linked securities manager LGT ILS Partners, continued to exhibit a lower loss ratio last year according to AM Best, while its latest financial report shows the gross premiums underwritten rose 23% in 2024 to approaching $207 million. While accident year losses were a little higher in 2024 than 2023, AM Best noted in its latest assessment that improvement has continued over time, saying that Lumen Re’s “loss ratio has decreased significantly since 2022.” Lumen Re continues to underwrite natural catastrophe exposed reinsurance, channelling that to entities in the LGT ILS Partners business structure, to efficiently originate opportunities for certain of the managers ILS funds. rating affirmation by AM Best, the agency explained that the Class 3B reinsurer has again renewed its status as a reciprocal jurisdiction reinsurance company in 37 US states for 2026 It’s now the third year in a row the LGT ILS Partners linked reinsurer has kept this status intact, which AM Best noted “reduces operational burden and costs” for Lumen Re.

item.title: US Coastal insurers secure $100m Chartwell Re cat bond at reduced pricing
item.description: As we later reported , the target size of the issuance remained the same but the sponsoring insurers, US Coastal Insurance Company and US Coastal Property & Casualty Insurance Company, were aiming to secure the reinsurance protection at reduced pricing, capitalising on strong execution in the cat bond market. The US Coastal insurers returned to the cat bond market earlier this month, looking to expand the role catastrophe bonds play in their reinsurance tower, with an initial target to secure $100 million of fully-collateralized reinsurance from this new Chartwell Re Series 2026-1 offering. So the Chartwell Re Series 2026-1 cat bond notes are now confirmed to provide $100 million of catastrophe reinsurance to the two US Coastal insurers on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis, across a three year term and covering named storm losses in the states of Alabama, Florida, Mississippi, New Jersey, New York, Rhode Island, and Texas.

item.title: More details on State Farm’s new $1.5bn Merna Re cat bonds, all annual aggregate protection
item.description: These Merna Re Enterprise II 2026-1 cat bond notes will provide State Farm with a $1.5 billion roughly three year source of collateralized reinsurance protection, running to maturity in early July 2029 When we learned about this deal we did not know the exact perils covered, although we assumed they would likely cover multiple perils on an indemnity basis as is typical of State Farm’s recent cat bonds, or whether the $1.5 billion of coverage was per-occurrence or annual aggregate. Now we have learned that both tranches of notes will provide State Farm with the roughly three years of reinsurance protection on an annual aggregate and indemnity trigger basis. We’ve now updated our previous article to reflect the correct name for the SPI that issued the new cat bonds for State Farm and amended our Deal Directory entry for the issuance to be Merna Re Enterprise II Ltd.

item.title: American Global Insurance secures quota share agreement with Lloyd’s syndicates
item.description: American Global Insurance, part of the American Global Insurance and Reinsurance Group (American Global Group), has secured a major quota share reinsurance partnership with two corporately owned Lloyd’s syndicates to back its healthcare indemnity insurance portfolio. Effective January 1, 2026, the participating Lloyd’s syndicates assumed 50% of premiums and losses for risks underwritten by the group’s principal subsidiary, American Global Insurance, Inc. According to the Commissioner of Insurance for the Modoc Nation, AGII is believed to be the first tribal insurance company to establish a reinsurance relationship with Lloyd’s syndicates for this type of coverage.

item.title: Insurers better prepared to deal with disease outbreaks since COVID: AM Best
item.description: The latest outbreaks are putting a renewed focus on the insurance implications of the spread of rare communicable diseases and the need for proactive risk management, according to AM Best’s commentary, “Insurers Better Prepared For Rare Disease Outbreak Amid Lessons From COVID-19.” Health experts have said that currently, neither virus poses an immediate global threat nor is expected to spread anywhere near the scale of COVID-19. Credit ratings agency AM Best finds that insurance companies are well-prepared to handle any direct impact from the recent outbreaks of the Ebola virus and Andes hantavirus, thanks in part to the “lessons learned” from the COVID-19 pandemic in coverage gaps and systemic vulnerabilities. Sridhar Manyem, Senior Director, Industry Research and Analytics, AM Best, commented, “Since the pandemic, insurers have increased investments in areas such as telehealth and digital or automated processes, as well as addressed policy ambiguities through re-underwriting.

item.title: Jencap Group names Kevin Hahn, Jr. EVP, National Casualty
item.description: Jencap Group LLC, a US wholesale insurance brokerage group and one of the nation’s largest wholesalers, has announced the promotion of Kevin Hahn, Jr. Prior to becoming part of Jencap Group LLC, Hahn spent nine years working in retail insurance brokerage across both regional and national firms, specialising in construction, development and real estate business. Joe Hayes, National Casualty Practice Leader at Jencap, commented: “Kevin’s journey has been one of the fastest in revenue growth of anyone who has ever assumed a wholesale broker position.

item.title: Hyundai to Recall Over 421,000 US Vehicles Over Software Brake Issue: NHTSA
item.description: Hyundai Motor is recalling 421,078 vehicles in the U.S. over a software error that may cause an unexpected application of brakes, the U.S. The recall covers certain 2025-2026 model Santa Cruz, Tucson, Tucson Hybrid, and Tucson Plug-In Hybrid Electric vehicles.

item.title: Saving Supply Chains From Climate Shocks Is a Lure for Investors
item.description: The intervention earlier this month is an example of how nascent technology can help protect the world’s $35 trillion global trade system , not just from geopolitical disruption and human error, but also from an increasingly unpredictable climate. Those threats are delivering new demand for adaptation strategies — everything from sensor manufacturers and climate risk analytics tools to insurance based on catastrophe modeling — and fueling a small but growing investment class offering unexpected returns. Advances in satellite imagery, more granular weather data, 5G networks and artificial intelligence are also helping to propel new climate tech start-ups.

item.title: Britain’s Navy Prepares to Clear Mines in Strait of Hormuz While Waiting for Peace Deal
item.description: Aboard the RFA Lyme Bay docked off the coast of Gibraltar, hundreds of British sailors are waiting to be deployed for a mine-clearing mission to the Strait of Hormuz that is still in doubt. in particular has drawn the ire of Trump, who has described Britain’s navy as “toys” and Prime Minister Keir Starmer as “not Winston Churchill.” At least 6,000 ships have been blocked from passing through the strait since the conflict began, Carns said. On the southern tip of the Iberian Peninsula, in the British Overseas Territory of Gibraltar, the U.K.’s Royal Navy is preparing to do that — but only once a peace agreement is reached.

item.title: War Overtakes Civil Unrest as Top Political Violence Exposure for Global Businesses
item.description: War has overtaken civil unrest as the political violence exposure that companies fear most, as conflicts in Europe and the Middle East disrupt global trade flows, strain political alliances, embolden adversarial powers and heighten risks to business assets, according to the Allianz Risk Barometer 2026 . War was identified as the political violence exposure most feared by companies, surpassing civil unrest to garner 53% of the votes in the 2026 Allianz Risk Barometer, climbing from 48% in 2025. Around 60% of respondents from Europe and Asia-Pacific see war as the top political risk and violence exposure, while civil unrest ranks No.

item.title: FBI Prepares for Increasing Drone Threats Disrupting World Cup
item.description: The FBI plans to deploy roughly 60 specially trained state and local police officers across venues for the FIFA World Cup to detect and electronically disable hostile drones as security officials warn that threats once associated with foreign battlefields are increasingly possible inside the US. “Every major public gathering, from a World Cup match to an America 250 celebration, is now a drone environment,” Devin Kowalski, an FBI assistant director who oversees the Critical Incident Response Group, said during the exercise. Officers from agencies including state police in New York and New Jersey, the New York City and Los Angeles police departments and the Miami-Dade Sheriff’s Office will operate counter-drone equipment under FBI supervision, according to a person familiar with the program who isn’t authorized to speak publicly.

item.title: Insurers warm to APAC nuclear boom as energy demand surges, reactor construction accelerates
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: New York Shipyard Blast That Killed 1, Injured More Than 30 Firefighters Under Investigation
item.description: Investigators were combing through a New York City shipyard on Saturday trying to find the cause of a fire and an explosion that killed one person and injured more than 30 firefighters and other first responders. Firefighters were called to the shipyard about 3:30 p.m. Less than an hour later, as firefighters and emergency medical personnel were working the scene, an explosion rocked the site, officials said.

item.title: Organizations Must Plan for Climate Tipping Points
item.description: Organizations must incorporate climate tipping points into risk planning as scientific focus shifts from if to when they'll occur. Finding a route through extreme uncertainty from climate tipping points is now urgent for organizations. It is critical that organizations and their stakeholders lift themselves out of the climate catastrophizing that tipping points may prompt.

item.title: Farmers files trademark for “Coverage on a Page”
item.description: Farmers Insurance Exchange filed a trademark application for “Farmers Coverage on a Page,” signaling a potential new branding initiative tied to insurance information and policy presentation. The trademark filing describes the offering as “insurance information” and “providing insurance information in the field of auto and home insurance.” The trademark name suggests Farmers may be developing a simplified coverage summary or customer-facing insurance explanation format designed to present policy information in a more concise manner. The application was filed on May 21, 2026 and covers insurance information services related to auto and home insurance.

item.title: «Los resultados importan, pero el liderazgo se construye en el cómo»
item.description: “El mundo empresarial ha evolucionado desde una orientación casi exclusiva a los resultados hacia un modelo en el que sigue importando el qué, pero cada vez tiene más peso el cómo”, explicó. El liderazgo ya no se mide solo por los resultados, ahora es igual de importante la cultura, los comportamientos y la forma de trabajar, afirma Roberto Muñoz, director general de Santander Seguros, quien compartió con las alumnas del curso de liderazgo femenino Next Generation Women in Insurance, impulsado por #RedEWI, su experiencia profesional y su visión sobre la evolución del liderazgo, la gestión de personas y la transformación de las organizaciones. A lo largo de la sesión, destacó cómo ha cambiado el concepto de liderazgo en las empresas y la creciente importancia de la cultura corporativa y de las personas.

item.title: China floods trigger US$110m in losses as claims top 33,000
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Inszone Insurance acquires Legacy Partners Insurance
item.description: Inszone Insurance acquired Michigan-based Legacy Partners Insurance , expanding its footprint in the Midwest and adding a Southeast Michigan agency focused on commercial, personal, and benefits insurance. Legacy Partners COO Cale Johnson will become General Counsel at Inszone. Founded in 2013 by Joe Graham, Legacy Partners operates from Harrison Township and grew through a combination of organic expansion and book acquisitions.

item.title: Long Ridge Equity Partners backs Ripjar
item.description: Ripjar , an AI-focused screening and financial crime compliance company, reported 40% annual recurring revenue growth over the past year and secured additional investment from Long Ridge Equity Partners . “Long Ridge is committed to backing category leaders and Ripjar continues to redefine the screening landscape. This additional capital reflects our commitment to Matt’s leadership and the team’s unique ability to solve this market’s evolving challenges.” – Jason Melton, Partner at Long Ridge.

item.title: iA Financial Group names Sébastien Mc Mahon Chief Economist
item.description: iA Financial Group appointed Sébastien Mc Mahon as Chief Economist, expanding his role within the organization. Mc Mahon previously served as Senior Economist and Chief Strategist at iA Global Asset Management. His ability to translate complex macroeconomic dynamics into clear, relevant insights strengthens iA Financial Group’s positioning as a trusted, forward looking financial institution in North America.” – Alain Bergeron, Executive Vice-President and Chief Investment Officer at iA Financial Group.

item.title: El costo-beneficio detrás de la continuidad operativa, la estrategia de Spin
item.description: Los respaldos, redundancia en la nube y los planes de continuidad ya no se justifican solo como inversiones técnicas: hoy necesitan demostrar su valor frente al negocio con una evaluación de costo–beneficio donde los indicadores de seguridad demuestren su impacto en la operación. La tendencia reciente es que, más que preguntar cuánto se gastó en seguridad, los directorios y áreas de negocio exigen demostrar qué riesgos se reducen y qué servicios siguen operando. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: El beneficio de las aseguradoras sube un 5% en los tres primeros meses de 2026
item.description: Las estadísticas de Icea muestran un ligero aumento de la siniestralidad media del conjunto de aseguradoras desde el 74,35% de las primas imputadas de negocio directo en marzo de 2025 al 75,34% al cierre de los tres primeros meses de este año. Con un resultado de la cuenta no técnica del 8,04% de las primas imputadas de negocio retenido en el primer trimestre del año (8,34% en el mismo periodo de 2025), las aseguradoras alcanzaron en el inicio del ejercicio un beneficio neto conjunto de 1.696 millones de euros, un 4,7% más que doce meses antes. En líneas generales, las aseguradoras inician el año con una ralentización en el incremento de sus resultados respecto a 2025, un ejercicio que se cerró con una mejora del 15% en las ganancias.

item.title: ¿Son caros los seguros en España? Un tercio de los consumidores cree que sí
item.description: La presión del coste de vida impacta con mayor intensidad en los consumidores españoles que en el resto de mercados europeos analizados en el ‘Estudio Europeo de Consumidores de Seguros 2026′, de Guidewire. En España y Francia, el 26% y el 32%, respectivamente, consideran que los productos del sector son caros. En otra de las cuestiones analizadas, España es el país con mayor proporción de consumidores favorables a que las aseguradoras recopilen información a través de sensores y dispositivos conectados : un 40% valora esta práctica de forma positiva, frente al 36% que la rechaza.

item.title: «El talento senior es absolutamente estratégico»
item.description: Sin embargo, en muchas ocasiones ese talento senior no se aprovecha plenamente o queda relegado a funciones menos estratégicas. Conversamos con ella sobre el valor del talento senior y sobre cómo la diversidad, entendida en toda su amplitud —incluyendo la experiencia generacional—, se ha convertido en un factor clave para la sostenibilidad y competitividad de las organizaciones. Para Olga García Pozo, profesional de Recursos Humanos con una larga trayectoria en el sector bancario y actual voluntaria senior en SECOT, estaba claro que todavía tenía mucho que aportar.

item.title: Insurance AI Is Booming. Fraud Losses Are Not Falling.
item.description: The Insurance Information Institute, citing the Coalition Against Insurance Fraud’s 2022 SIU Benchmarking Study, found that SIU staffing grew just 1.4 percent from 2021 to 2022. Insurers have spent the last three years teaching AI to catch fraud. Most conversations about fraud detection miss this: the documents are often real.

item.title: Tower elevates Michael Skeens to chief operating officer
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden hires FM veteran Manikandan Krishnamoorthy as regional director for risk and engineering advisory
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending May 24th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending May 24th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending May 24th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Beyond the canvas: APAC fine art and specie insurance boom fuelled by rising risks, shifting wealth patterns
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: South Korea’s DB Insurance set to complete Fortegra acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Nationwide expands retirement product leadership team
item.description: Nationwide Retirement Solutions announced that Adam Lacks has been appointed associate vice president (AVP) of Product Development for government markets, effective April 27. In this role, he will report to Hutch Schafer and lead product strategy, design and development for the Retirement Solutions business supported on the DCdirect recordkeeping platform. Lacks will oversee the development of new products, features and capabilities designed to keep Nationwide’s retirement plans business aligned with and ahead of the evolving public sector defined contribution marketplace.

item.title: Energetic Capital expands credit insurance capabilities and capacity
item.description: Energetic Capital announced renewed credit insurance agreements with SCOR and additional capacity from Lloyd's markets led by OAK Global, expanding its underwriting capabilities for clean energy and infrastructure projects. “We see a compelling opportunity to deploy (re)insurance capacity through Energetic Capital’s underwriting platform. With SCOR’s continued support and new capacity from Lloyd’s led by OAK Global, we are well positioned to scale our underwriting and bring flexible solutions to a broader set of infrastructure opportunities.” – Nathan Maggiotto, CEO of Energetic Capital.

item.title: Inter Miami CF partners with Marsh
item.description: Inter Miami CF announced a partnership with Marsh, naming the insurance broker the club’s commercial insurance broker and official partner. Under the agreement, Marsh will provide insurance brokerage services for Inter Miami CF’s operations, including Nu Stadium and the broader Miami Freedom Park development. Marsh will also use Inter Miami CF hospitality assets and matchday experiences for client engagement and business development activities in South Florida.

item.title: Inszone acquires Taylor Insurance Agency in Texas
item.description: Inszone Insurance announced the acquisition of Kevin Taylor Insurance Agency, LLC, operating as Taylor Insurance Agency, expanding its personal lines presence in San Antonio, Texas. “I grew this agency on my own, but I knew I needed Inszone to get to the next level.” – Kevin Taylor, owner of Taylor Insurance Agency. Founded in 2011 as a captive agency before transitioning to an independent model in 2014, Taylor Insurance Agency built a large personal lines portfolio focused on individually generated business.

item.title: Japan’s big 3 make waves globally
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Efectibank recibe licencia en Perú y Klar compra Yave
item.description: Financiera Efectiva migra a Efectibank, nuevo banco digital La empresa financiera peruana recibió la licencia de funcionamiento para operar como banco digital en el país andino, su nueva apuesta por ampliar sus facultades y competir en el segmento. De esta forma, Efectibank se convierte en el primer banco digital peruano con presencia física. Cajas municipales preparan billetera para competir en pagos digitales de Perú Las cajas municipales en Perú avanzan en el desarrollo de una billetera interoperable llamada Toque , con la que buscan ganar participación en el negocio de pagos digitales frente a bancos y fintechs.

item.title: Manulife Announces Executive Leadership Changes
item.description: Source: Manulife Hong Kong.) Manulife (Toronto) has announced changes to its executive and senior leadership teams across Canada, Hong Kong, AI and data, and technology and operations as the company executes its refreshed enterprise strategy. “These important leadership changes ensure we have the right capabilities, both at the enterprise level and in our key markets of Canada and Hong Kong, to achieve our bold ambition and deliver against our new strategic priorities,” comments Phil Witherington, President and CEO, Manulife. I am confident that we have the right leadership team to deliver on Manulife’s next chapter of long-term growth.” The changes are effective July 1, 2026.

item.title: Banorte Extends Risk Management with AI Signals
item.description: IIR met Abraham Izquierdo, Managing Director of Treasury Risk, Banorte, at SAS Innovate 2026 in Grapevine, Texas, where SAS introduced him as a customer whose work in banking risk management had relevance beyond banking. Izquierdo described how Banorte is using SAS technology, core banking data and in-house machine learning to connect liquidity-risk monitoring with external sentiment signals. “One of those is risk management.” From Balance Sheet Modeling to Broader Risk Analytics Banorte’s AI-enabled liquidity-risk initiative builds on a broader risk analytics program that has included balance sheet modeling and interest-rate risk management, areas where the institution has used SAS technology to model banking-book positions and analyze the behavior of assets, liabilities and hedges.

item.title: Guardian Builds AI-Ready Talent for Enterprise Change
item.description: (Guardian Life reception area with views of the High Line at Hudson Yards. Image source: Perkins & Will.) Insurance technology professionals had little reason to expect the pace of change to slow. AI has introduced a new degree of acceleration, compressing what had been a steady evolution of skills into a more urgent reconsideration of technology talent, operating models and business accountability.

item.title: Calling Cops Delay Doesn’t Wreck UM Claim, West Virginia High Court Rules
item.description: But that’s not enough to negate a claim, the West Virginia Supreme Court of Appeals decided this week. “… We conclude that, under the clear and unambiguous language of the subject policy, West Virginia National was required to prove that its investigative interests were prejudiced by petitioners’ failure to report the accident to a police, peace, or judicial officer within twenty-four hours,” the high court wrote in its May 21 opinion . The couple did report the crash to the insurance company within four days, and West Virginia National began its own investigation of the claim.
